strengths: Strong technical foundation with MIT CS degree and 19+ years of full-stack engineering experience across web, mobile, infrastructure, and systems domains
Demonstrated technical leadership through multiple staff roles, open-source projects (GISCollective, fluent-asserts, Trial), and community mentoring (workshops, code dojos, courses)
Currently engaged with AI infrastructure and developer tooling at re:cinq, showing meaningful engagement with cutting-edge AI technologies and agentic workflows
concerns: AI expertise is recent (April 2025–present, ~14 months) focused on infrastructure and tooling rather than AI product strategy or platform differentiation—insufficient for 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products'

Career trajectory is primarily engineering-focused (individual contributor, staff engineer) with limited evidence of principal-level strategic advisory work or directing cloud/AI strategy differentiation
No Master's degree or PhD in AI/ML; lacks the formal advanced credentials the role prefers for establishing recognized AI expertise
response.justification: The candidate has a strong foundational career—MIT degree, 19 years of engineering breadth, multiple staff-level roles—and has engaged with AI infrastructure for 14 months. However, the role specifically requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI.' This candidate's AI work is recent and infrastructure-focused (MCP frameworks, developer tooling) rather than AI product strategy or platform differentiation. The resume demonstrates solid technical execution and leadership but lacks the proven strategic expertise in AI that a principal engineer in this specialization must bring.
